DECLARATION OF ROBERTS S. FLETCHER IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS NOTICE MOTION AND MOTION TO STAY THE PROCEEDINGS (TRANSACTION ID # 100004006) FILED BY DEFENDANT CHEUNG, ADORA CHEUNG, AARON

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS AARON CHEUNG AND ADORA CHEUNG'S MOTION TO STAY THE PROCEEDINGS/THIS CASE IS DENIED. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFFS WERE EMPLOYED BY THE CHEUNGS. IF PROVED, THIS ALLEGATION ESTABLISHES THAT THE CHEUNGS HAVE LIABILITY SEPARATE AND APART FROM HOMEJOY AND THUS HOMEJOY, WHILE POTENTIALLY JOINTLY LIABLE FOR THE SAME RELIEF AS SOUGHT AGAINST THE CHEUNGS, IS NOT AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY PER CCP 389. CCP 404.5 AND CRC 3.515 ARE INAPPLICABLE SINCE THERE IS NO PENDING PETITION FOR COORDINATION. THE BANKRUPTCY STAY PROVISIONS OF THE US CODE ARE LIKEWISE INAPPLICABLE SINCE THE CHEUNGS HAVE NOT FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY AND THOSE PROVISIONS DO NOT EXTEND TO CO-DEFENDANTS OF A PARTY WHO DID FILE FOR BANKRUPTCY. WHILE THIS COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO ISSUE A STAY IN THE INTERESTS OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY, THE CHEUNGS FAIL TO EXPLAIN HOW THE STAY THEY REQUEST WOULD SERVE JUDICIAL ECONOMY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Continued To Apr-09-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Motion And Motion To Stay The Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Aaron Cheung and Adora Cheung's motion to stay the proceedings/this case is denied. The second amended complaint alleges that plaintiffs were employed by the Cheungs. If proved, this allegation establishes that the Cheungs have liability separate and apart from Homejoy and thus Homejoy, while potentially jointly liable for the same relief as sought against the Cheungs, is not an indispensable party per CCP 389. CCP 404.5 and CRC 3.515 are inapplicable since there is no pending petition for coordination. The bankruptcy stay provisions of the US Code are likewise inapplicable since the Cheungs have not filed for bankruptcy and those provisions do not extend to co-defendants of a party who did file for bankruptcy. While this court has the authority to issue a stay in the interests of judicial economy, the Cheungs fail to explain how the stay they request would serve judicial economy. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
